papers.title: Neu-PiG: Neural Preconditioned Grids for Fast Dynamic Surface Reconstruction on Long Sequences
papers.summary: Temporally consistent surface reconstruction of dynamic 3D objects from unstructured point cloud data remains challenging, especially for very long sequences. papers.title: WHOLE: World-Grounded Hand-Object Lifted from Egocentric Videos
papers.summary: Egocentric manipulation videos are highly challenging due to severe occlusions during interactions and frequent object entries and exits from the camera view as the person moves. papers.title: Solaris: Building a Multiplayer Video World Model in Minecraft
papers.summary: Existing action-conditioned video generation models (video world models) are limited to single-agent perspectives, failing to capture the multi-agent interactions of real-world environments. papers.summary: The reliability of multilingual Large Language Model (LLM) evaluation is currently compromised by the inconsistent quality of translated benchmarks. papers.summary: Sumerian transliteration is a conventional system for representing a scholar's interpretation of a tablet in the Latin script. papers.summary: Advances in Generative AI (GenAI) have led to the development of various protection strategies to prevent the unauthorized use of images. papers.summary: We study reasoning for accessing world knowledge stored in a language model's parameters. papers.summary: Open-source native GUI agents still lag behind closed-source systems on long-horizon navigation tasks. papers.title: Surrogate models for Rock-Fluid Interaction: A Grid-Size-Invariant Approach
papers.summary: Modelling rock-fluid interaction requires solving a set of partial differential equations (PDEs) to predict the flow behaviour and the reactions of the fluid with the rock on the interfaces. Conventional high-fidelity numerical models require a high resolution to obtain reliable results, resulting in huge computational expense. papers.title: LiCQA : A Lightweight Complex Question Answering System

papers.title: Learning and Naming Subgroups with Exceptional Survival Characteristics
papers.summary: In many applications, it is important to identify subpopulations that survive longer or shorter than the rest of the population. papers.summary: In recent years, a standard computational pathology workflow has emerged where whole slide images are cropped into tiles, these tiles are processed using a foundation model, and task-specific models are built using the resulting representations. papers.title: DySCO: Dynamic Attention-Scaling Decoding for Long-Context LMs
papers.summary: Understanding and reasoning over long contexts is a crucial capability for language models (LMs). Although recent models support increasingly long context windows, their accuracy often deteriorates as input length grows. papers.summary: Mixed-Integer Programs (MIPs) are NP-hard optimization models that arise in a broad range of decision-making applications, including finance, logistics, energy systems, and network design. papers.summary: Arbitrary-Scale SR (ASISR) remains fundamentally limited by cross-scale distribution shift: once the inference scale leaves the training range, noise, blur, and artifacts accumulate sharply. papers.summary: The inability of Large Language Models (LLMs) to modulate their personality expression in response to evolving dialogue dynamics hinders their performance in complex, interactive contexts. papers.summary: Most contemporary neural learning systems rely on epoch-based optimization and repeated access to historical data, implicitly assuming reversible computation. papers.summary: Unified conditional image generation remains difficult because different tasks depend on fundamentally different internal representations. papers.title: When AI Writes, Whose Voice Remains? Quantifying Cultural Marker Erasure Across World English Varieties in Large Language Models
papers.summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used to ``professionalize'' workplace communication, often at the cost of linguistic identity. papers.summary: Medical vision-language pretraining increasingly relies on medical reports as large-scale supervisory signals; however, raw reports often exhibit substantial stylistic heterogeneity, variable length, and a considerable amount of image-irrelevant content. papers.summary: Neural networks (NNs) are pervasive across various domains but often lack interpretability. papers.summary: Chest X-ray (CXR) interpretation is hindered by the long-tailed distribution of pathologies and the open-world nature of clinical environments. papers.summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have revolutionized inference across diverse natural language tasks, with larger models performing better but at higher computational costs. papers.summary: Alignment and adaptation in large language models heavily rely on reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF); yet, theoretical understanding of its generalisability remains premature, especially when the learned reward could shift, and the KL control is estimated and clipped. papers.title: Improving Implicit Discourse Relation Recognition with Natural Language Explanations from LLMs
papers.summary: Implicit Discourse Relation Recognition (IDRR) remains a challenging task due to the requirement for deep semantic understanding in the absence of explicit discourse markers. papers.title: Learning from Yesterday's Error: An Efficient Online Learning Method for Traffic Demand Prediction
papers.summary: Accurately predicting short-term traffic demand is critical for intelligent transportation systems. papers.title: Offline Reasoning for Efficient Recommendation: LLM-Empowered Persona-Profiled Item Indexing
papers.summary: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) offer new opportunities for recommender systems by capturing the nuanced semantics of user interests and item characteristics through rich semantic understanding and contextual reasoning. papers.title: The ASIR Courage Model: A Phase-Dynamic Framework for Truth Transitions in Human and AI Systems

papers.summary: The reasoning process of Large Language Models (LLMs) is often plagued by hallucinations and missing facts in question-answering tasks. papers.summary: The growing integration of distributed photovoltaics (PVs) into active distribution networks (ADNs) has exacerbated operational challenges, making it imperative to coordinate diverse equipment to mitigate voltage violations and enhance power quality. papers.title: SurGo-R1: Benchmarking and Modeling Contextual Reasoning for Operative Zone in Surgical Video
papers.summary: Minimally invasive surgery has dramatically improved patient operative outcomes, yet identifying safe operative zones remains challenging in critical phases, requiring surgeons to integrate visual cues, procedural phase, and anatomical context under high cognitive load. papers.summary: A central challenge in scientific machine learning (ML) is the correct representation of physical systems governed by multi-regime behaviours. papers.title: DWA-KD: Dual-Space Weighting and Time-Warped Alignment for Cross-Tokenizer Knowledge Distillation
papers.summary: Knowledge Distillation (KD) has emerged as a crucial technique for compressing Large Language Models (LLMs). papers.title: CCCaption: Dual-Reward Reinforcement Learning for Complete and Correct Image Captioning
papers.summary: Image captioning remains a fundamental task for vision language understanding, yet ground-truth supervision still relies predominantly on human-annotated references. papers.title: Sparsity Induction for Accurate Post-Training Pruning of Large Language Models
papers.summary: Large language models have demonstrated capabilities in text generation, while their increasing parameter scales present challenges in computational and memory efficiency. papers.summary: Bangla-English code-mixing is widespread across South Asian social media, yet resources for implicit meaning identification in this setting remain scarce. papers.title: Goodness-of-Fit Tests for Latent Class Models with Ordinal Categorical Data

papers.summary: We study stochastic gradient descent (SGD) for composite optimization problems with $N$ sequential operators subject to perturbations in both the forward and backward passes. papers.summary: We introduce a diagonalization-based optimization for Linear Echo State Networks (ESNs) that reduces the per-step computational complexity of reservoir state updates from O(N^2) to O(N). papers.title: Partial Soft-Matching Distance for Neural Representational Comparison with Partial Unit Correspondence
papers.summary: Representational similarity metrics typically force all units to be matched, making them susceptible to noise and outliers common in neural representations. papers.summary: The remarkable performance of modern AI systems has been driven by unprecedented scales of data, computation, and energy -- far exceeding the resources required by human intelligence. papers.title: Toward Manifest Relationality in Transformers via Symmetry Reduction
papers.summary: Transformer models contain substantial internal redundancy arising from coordinate-dependent representations and continuous symmetries, in model space and in head space, respectively. papers.title: Robustness of Deep ReLU Networks to Misclassification of High-Dimensional Data
papers.summary: We present a theoretical study of the robustness of parameterized networks to random input perturbations. Specifically, we analyze local robustness at a given network input by quantifying the probability that a small additive random perturbation of the input leads to misclassification. For deep networks with rectified linear units, we derive lower bounds on local robustness in terms of the input dimensionality and the total number of network units.

papers.summary: Neural networks have long strived to emulate the learning capabilities of the human brain.
